transcript.text: Hello, this is a video that I've been wanting to do for a long time now, and it's how we should look at history.
We should look at history as though it's us.
I think we should look at history, I think we should plug in the idea of reincarnation into history.
Looking at the past as though it literally was us in a past life.
And if you can't believe in reincarnation for whatever reason, to look at the past as though it's us in the sense that it's how we would act if we were in that situation.
Because the world was a lot different back in the olden times, before there was a printing press and electricity and guns.
It was kind of like how we would act if tomorrow, say there was a pole shift and there was no more electricity or gasoline for cars and we all had to fend for ourselves.
Maybe we would all revert back to survival mode and start killing each other for food.
And that's kind of how it was in the past.

So to look at the past and see how they had slavery or even situations where women didn't have any power, instead of looking at them like they were incomprehensible or something that was so far beneath you you can't relate to it.
They're like just crazy to actually try to imagine that they're just normal people who want the best for everybody in the long run but they have to take care of themselves first.
And then explain how it was like that because there were, I mean we had some crazy, we had some crazy ways about us in the past.
For example, how about the Vikings?
They came down from, in 790 they came down from Scandinavia and just ran through the fields just killing people for fun basically and just taking their stuff and they would even kill each other.
There was a story of back home, some little Viking kid got like beaten by some other kid in a competition or something, like a rock throwing competition.
So he got jealous, so he went and got his axe and killed the kid with the axe and then his mother was like, ah, you're a good boy, you're going to be a good Viking one day, you know?
And it's like how, to try and contemplate how people could have been like that.
Maybe they were just desperate and they had just lost all hope of, of society because they just, I guess maybe they had too many good years in what's normally really freezing Scandinavia
And they just overpopulated so there was not enough people, not enough land for the people so they had, they got kicked, they got like killed and kicked out
And so they felt like they just lost their land so they might as well just go crazy and rampage, you know?
That's kind of the thing.
